tasks.subject: [interactive-card-focus-navigation] [P1] Widen activeInteraction type to expose full ToolCallState
tasks.description:
## Objective

Widen the `activeInteraction` return type in `useChatSession` to expose the full `ToolCallState` object instead of the current narrow shape (`{ toolCallId, interactiveType }`). This gives the input zone all the data it needs to render ToolApproval and QuestionPrompt without a redundant lookup.

## Background

The `pendingInteractions` derivation in `use-chat-session.ts` already produces `ToolCallState` objects that contain `toolCallId`, `interactiveType`, `toolName`, `input`, `questions`, `answers`, `timeoutMs`, and `status`. The current code narrows this to only `{ toolCallId, interactiveType }` before exposing it. We need the full object.

## Implementation Steps

1. Open `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/use-chat-session.ts`
2. Find where `activeInteraction` is derived from `pendingInteractions`. It currently picks only `toolCallId` and `interactiveType`.
3. Change it to return the full `ToolCallState` object (or `null`):

```typescript
// Before (narrow type):
const activeInteraction = pendingInteractions[0]
  ? { toolCallId: pendingInteractions[0].toolCallId, interactiveType: pendingInteractions[0].interactiveType }
  : null;

// After (full ToolCallState):
const activeInteraction = pendingInteractions[0] || null;
```

4. Update the return type annotation if one exists to use `ToolCallState | null` instead of the narrow shape.
5. Check `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/chat-types.ts` for an `ActiveInteraction` type. If it exists as a narrow type, widen it to `ToolCallState` or create a type alias:

```typescript
import type { ToolCallState } from '@dorkos/shared/types';

/** The currently active interactive tool awaiting user input. */
export type ActiveInteraction = ToolCallState;
```

6. Check all consumers of `activeInteraction` in `ChatPanel.tsx` to ensure they still compile. The widened type is a superset — existing field accesses (`toolCallId`, `interactiveType`) remain valid.
7. Verify the `useInteractiveShortcuts` hook still receives the correct shape. It currently expects `{ type: 'approval' | 'question', toolCallId: string }`. The `interactiveType` field from `ToolCallState` uses the string `'approval' | 'question'` — confirm the field name mapping. If `useInteractiveShortcuts` expects `type` but `ToolCallState` uses `interactiveType`, update the mapping in `ChatPanel` where the hook is called.

## Files Changed

- `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/use-chat-session.ts` — Widen activeInteraction return
- `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/model/chat-types.ts` — Update ActiveInteraction type (if exists)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `activeInteraction` exposes `toolName`, `input`, `questions`, `answers`, `timeoutMs` in addition to `toolCallId` and `interactiveType`
- [ ] All existing consumers of `activeInteraction` compile without errors
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes
- [ ] Existing tests pass: `pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/`
tasks.activeForm: Widening activeInteraction type to full ToolCallState

tasks.subject: [interactive-card-focus-navigation] [P2] Thread interactive props from ChatPanel to ChatInputContainer
tasks.description:
## Objective

Connect the ChatPanel to ChatInputContainer by threading the `activeInteraction`, `focusedOptionIndex`, `onToolRef`, and `onToolDecided` props. This wires up the mode switching added in Task 2.1.

## Implementation

Edit `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/ChatPanel.tsx`:

ChatPanel already has access to `activeInteraction` from `useChatSession`, `focusedOptionIndex` from `useToolShortcuts`, `handleToolRef` (the ref callback), and `markToolCallResponded` (the decided callback). These need to be passed down to `ChatInputContainer`.

```typescript
<ChatInputContainer
  // ... all existing props ...
  activeInteraction={activeInteraction}
  focusedOptionIndex={focusedOptionIndex}
  onToolRef={handleToolRef}
  onToolDecided={() => {
    if (activeInteraction) {
      markToolCallResponded(activeInteraction.toolCallId);
    }
  }}
/>
```

The `onToolDecided` callback wraps `markToolCallResponded` to extract the toolCallId from the current `activeInteraction`. This matches the existing pattern used when the interactive card was rendered in the stream.

## Verifying the Wiring

After this task, the full pipeline works:
1. `useChatSession` provides `activeInteraction` (full `ToolCallState`)
2. `ChatPanel` threads it to `ChatInputContainer`
3. `ChatInputContainer` switches to interactive mode and renders ToolApproval/QuestionPrompt
4. The imperative handle is attached via `onToolRef`
5. `useToolShortcuts` in ChatPanel drives keyboard navigation
6. When the user acts, `onToolDecided` marks the tool call as responded

## Files Changed

- `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/ChatPanel.tsx` — Pass interactive props to ChatInputContainer

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `ChatInputContainer` receives all four new props from `ChatPanel`
- [ ] When a pending interaction exists, the input zone shows the interactive card
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts (Enter for approve, Escape for deny, arrows for options) work correctly
- [ ] After acting on an interaction, the input zone returns to normal mode
- [ ] Draft text is preserved across the mode switch
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes
tasks.activeForm: Threading interactive props from ChatPanel to ChatInputContainer

tasks.subject: [interactive-card-focus-navigation] [P3] Right-align Kbd hints and standardize option spacing in QuestionPrompt
tasks.description:
## Objective Move Kbd badges from inline after the label text to right-aligned within the row using `ml-auto`. Also update option container spacing from `space-y-0.5` to `space-y-1`. Remove the `isActive` conditional on Kbd visibility since when rendered in the input zone, hints are always visible. ## Current OptionRow Pattern (Single-select) In `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/QuestionPrompt.tsx`, the `renderQuestionContent` function (around line 291): ```tsx ``` ## New OptionRow Pattern (Single-select) ```tsx ``` Key changes: 1. Label gets `flex items-center` layout 2. Kbd badge moves AFTER description, uses `ml-auto shrink-0` to push it to the right edge 3. The `isActive` conditional on Kbd visibility is removed — hints are always visible 4. Description stays inline between label text and Kbd ## Apply to All Three OptionRow Locations The same pattern change must be applied in three places in `renderQuestionContent`: 1. **Single-select options** (RadioGroup) — around line 280-305 2. **Multi-select options** (Checkbox group) — around line 312-346 3. **"Other" free-text option** (`renderOtherOption` function) — around line 218-265 For the "Other" option, the Kbd badge needs to move into the label line: ```tsx // Current: // New: ``` ## Option Container Spacing Update the container spacing from `space-y-0.5` to `space-y-1`: ```tsx // Single-select: // Multi-select:
``` Currently both use `space-y-0.5`. Change to `space-y-1` for slightly more breathing room. ## Files Changed - `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/QuestionPrompt.tsx` — Reposition Kbd badges, update spacing ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Kbd badges appear at the right edge of option rows (via `ml-auto shrink-0`) - [ ] Kbd badges are always visible (no `isActive` conditional) - [ ] Labels use `flex items-center` layout - [ ] Description text appears between label and Kbd badge - [ ] Option container spacing is `space-y-1` (not `space-y-0.5`) - [ ] All three option row locations updated: single-select, multi-select, and "Other" - [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes
tasks.activeForm: Right-aligning Kbd hints and standardizing option spacing

tasks.subject: [interactive-card-focus-navigation] [P4] Run full test suite and verify all acceptance criteria
tasks.description:
## Objective

Run the complete test suite, fix any remaining issues, and verify all 15 acceptance criteria from the specification.

## Steps

1. Run full test suite:
```bash
pnpm test -- --run
```

2. Run type checking:
```bash
pnpm typecheck
```

3. Run linting:
```bash
pnpm lint
```

4. Fix any failures discovered by the above.

5. Verify each acceptance criterion from the spec (Section 9):

- [ ] AC1: When a tool approval arrives, the input zone transforms to show the approval card with a crossfade animation
- [ ] AC2: When a question prompt arrives, the input zone transforms to show the question form with a crossfade animation
- [ ] AC3: The message stream shows a compact "Waiting for approval..." or "Answering questions..." placeholder with a spinning indicator instead of the full interactive card
- [ ] AC4: All input-related elements (file chips, queue, autocomplete, status section) are hidden during interactive mode
- [ ] AC5: The user's draft text is preserved when entering interactive mode and restored when the interaction resolves
- [ ] AC6: The textarea is re-focused after draft restoration
- [ ] AC7: Keyboard shortcuts work correctly: Enter/Escape for approval, arrows/digits/Enter for questions
- [ ] AC8: Multi-question flows use Back/Next buttons with a step indicator instead of tabs
- [ ] AC9: Kbd hints appear after labels, right-aligned via ml-auto
- [ ] AC10: Option row spacing uses space-y-1 consistently
- [ ] AC11: Up/Down arrows navigate options even when the "Other" textarea is focused
- [ ] AC12: After interaction resolves, the input zone smoothly crossfades back to normal mode and the stream shows CompactResultRow
- [ ] AC13: History replay (pre-answered questions, already-decided approvals) renders CompactResultRow directly in the stream with no input zone involvement
- [ ] AC14: Multiple pending interactions are queued — input zone shows the oldest pending one, resolves it, then shows the next
- [ ] AC15: Existing tests continue to pass after updates for new rendering context

6. Check dev playground for visual verification if showcases render interactive tools. If `apps/client/src/dev/showcases/MessageShowcases.tsx` renders ToolApproval or QuestionPrompt inline, it may need the `inputZoneToolCallId` context. Update if needed.

## Files Changed

- Any files with test failures or lint issues discovered during verification
- Potentially `apps/client/src/dev/showcases/MessageShowcases.tsx` if showcases need updates

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `pnpm test -- --run` passes with zero failures
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes
- [ ] All 15 spec acceptance criteria verified
tasks.activeForm: Running full test suite and verifying acceptance criteria
